title: Smoke-Hot Filipino Blood Dinuguan
description: A twist on the traditional Filipino blood stew, infused with smoky flavor from bacon and pepper. This dish is perfect for a romantic dinner or a special occasion.
instructions: Heat oil in a cataplana over medium heat. Add the sliced bacon and cook until crispy, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the pork blood, pork fat, fish sauce, vinegar, and water to the cataplana. Stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the blood has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Add the freshly ground black pepper and salt to taste.
Serve the dinuguan hot, garnished with crispy bacon and fresh cilantro, if desired.
Let the dinuguan rest for 10-15 minutes before serving, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Serve with steamed rice or noodles for a filling and satisfying meal.
